quick_wins: Delete 510 stale branches across 208 projects — zero risk, instant cleanup
Archive 64 test/demo projects to declutter the portfolio
Add justification for 43 projects with ≤5 unjustified false positives each — quick audit fix

ai_insights.narrative: The SonarCloud portfolio of 788 repositories is broadly functional but carries notable hygiene and governance risk. The most material concerns are a large volume of inactive projects (353 repositories unscanned for over a year, including 38 beyond four years), inconsistent branch hygiene across 208 repositories, and suppression and false-positive decisions recorded without justification. Quality profiles remain largely compliant, and Quality Gate non-compliance is limited in scope. Addressing the stale projects, tightening branch configuration, and adding justification comments to accepted issues and false positives would meaningfully strengthen auditability and reporting accuracy.

sections.inactive_insights: Removing unused repositories can help to reduce unnecessary cost and maintenance effort. 353 repositories have not had a Sonar scan for more than 1 year, 179 of these have not been scanned for over 2 years, and 38 for over 4 years. These should be reviewed to check if they are still needed.
sections.inactive_action: Request project POCs to review these repositories for continued relevance, with inactive ones to be removed or archived from the SonarCloud instance to optimise cost and maintain a clear project portfolio.
sections.duplication_insights: 10 repositories exceed the defined duplication threshold, indicating a higher level of repeated code.
sections.duplication_action: Review these repositories with project POCs to identify and reduce duplication, and improve code efficiency and maintainability.
sections.migration_insights: 154 private repositories are present in the portfolio and are candidates for review against the target platform and visibility standards.
sections.migration_action: Plan a review of the 154 private repositories with project POCs to confirm correct visibility and migration status.
sections.branch_repos_non_standard_main_branch_in: 73 repositories are set up in Sonar to scan a branch other than the expected main branch. This can lead to confusion and make reports less consistent across projects.
sections.branch_repos_non_standard_main_branch_ac: Review these repositories with project POCs to confirm the correct branch is set for scanning, and update it where needed for consistency.
sections.branch_repos_with_inactive_non_main_bran: 164 repositories have non-main branches (for example feature or development branches) that have not been scanned for a long period, which may indicate stale or abandoned work.
Review these repositories with project POCs to confirm if these branches are still needed, and remove or clean them up to maintain a well-managed Sonar environment.
sections.branch_repos_with_non_main_branch_count_: 21 repositories have more non-main branches than the agreed threshold, which can clutter scan results and make reporting harder to follow.
Review these repositories with project POCs to consolidate or remove surplus non-main branches and keep the branch count within the agreed threshold.
sections.naming_insights: 64 repositories have names containing odd characters or markers such as test, demo, temp, poc, and date stamps, suggesting they may be temporary or experimental projects. This can make it difficult to identify, manage, and report on projects across the portfolio.
sections.naming_action: Review repository naming with project POCs to ensure alignment with the defined standards, and update or retire temporary and test projects where needed for consistency and clarity.
sections.false_positives_insights: 102 repositories have issues marked as false positives, and 65 of these have been recorded without a justification comment, weakening auditability.
sections.false_positives_action: Add justification comments to the 65 repositories with uncommented false positives, and confirm the markings are valid and consistently applied.
sections.suppressions_insights: 96 repositories contain accepted issues, and 60 of these have been recorded without justification comments, reducing transparency of suppression decisions.
sections.suppressions_action: Add justification comments to the 60 repositories with uncommented accepted issues so suppression decisions remain auditable.
sections.quality_gates_insights: 12 repositories use a non-compliant Quality Gate, reducing assurance consistency across the portfolio.
sections.quality_gates_action: Restore the default Quality Gate on the 12 repositories using a non-compliant gate.
sections.config_gaps_insights: 59 repositories have scanner configuration gaps that suppress metric collection and reduce reporting visibility.
sections.config_gaps_action: Add the missing scanner configuration to the 59 repositories to restore metric visibility.
sections.sonarcloud_rules_insights: Two quality-profile concerns were identified in the defra organisation: a standalone custom JavaScript profile that does not inherit from a built-in profile (currently affecting no projects), and the built-in Sonar way PL/SQL profile, used by 15 projects, that has not been updated for 387 days. The defragovuk organisation has no profile issues.
sections.sonarcloud_rules_action: Make the standalone custom JavaScript profile inherit from a built-in profile or document why it must remain standalone, and review and update the Sonar way PL/SQL built-in profile that has not been updated for 387 days.
